steps.title: Join two main clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form one independent clause about trying to improve NiCoCr. The words after the blank form another independent clause about the resulting alloy being unsuitable.

steps.title: Reject run-ons and splices
steps.content: Choice A fuses the clauses with no connector. Choice B uses but without the expected comma for these longer clauses. Choice C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice.

steps.title: Use comma plus but
steps.content: Choice D supplies (Ru), but, correctly joining the two independent clauses and signaling the contrast between the intended improvement and the unsuitable result. The answer is D.
